forecasts.reasoning: The forecast horizon spans a 33-day window from May 13 to June 14, 2025. Based on the historical frequency of interactions since Trump took office—a call on February 12, an in-person meeting on February 28, and another call on March 19—Trump and Zelenskyy have been communicating directly via phone or meeting approximately every 2 to 4 weeks. Given the high-stakes nature of the ongoing negotiations to halt Russian aggression and align ceasefire requests, continued regular communication between the principals is highly likely. Even though the February 28 meeting was described as 'disastrous,' the subsequent March 19 call was positive and resulted in a 30-day ceasefire on energy attacks, showing that their direct line of communication remains highly active. Modeling this as a Poisson process with an average rate of one call every 4–5 weeks yields a probability of at least one call in a 33-day window of roughly 55-65%. Given Trump's penchant for personal diplomacy but also the possibility of delegating to envoys or talks stalling, a 61% estimate balances the high frequency of recent calls with the volatility of the relationship.
